info.name: CreatorOS Auth
info.description: Auth business logic endpoints: register (auto-provisions Wallet + Free Subscription + 20 credits), login, refresh-token rotation, logout, forgot/reset password, email verification. Run 'Get CSRF Token' first — it stores both the CSRF cookie and a {{csrfToken}} collection variable that later requests send back as the x-csrf-token header. Postman's cookie jar carries the session/refresh cookies automatically between requests as long as they all hit {{baseUrl}}. Password reset / email verification tokens aren't returned in the API response (they're 'emailed') — copy the raw token out of the `npm run dev` console log (search for '[email:stub]') and paste it into the request body.

request.description: Sets the csrf_token cookie and returns the same value in the body. Run this first, and again any time csrfToken looks stale.

Creates the user, then atomically provisions: Wallet (balance 0) -> Free Subscription -> +20 credit Wallet Transaction (balance -> 20). Sets session + refresh cookies. 409 if the email is already taken.

401 invalid_credentials on wrong password. After 5 failed attempts in a row, returns 423 account_locked with an increasing-delay lockout. 429 rate_limited if hammered (20/15min per IP, 10/15min per email).

Uses the refresh_token cookie (no body/CSRF needed). Rotates to a new refresh token + a new 15-min session token. Replaying an already-rotated refresh token returns 401 refresh_token_reuse_detected and revokes the whole token family (forces full re-login).
Revokes the current refresh token and clears both cookies.

Always returns {ok:true} whether or not the email exists (no account enumeration). If it exists, a reset link is logged to the dev server console — copy the `token` query param from there into the Reset Password request below.
Single-use token, 1hr expiry. On success, revokes ALL of the user's refresh tokens (force logout everywhere).
Verification is optional and never blocks login — this just flips users.emailVerified. Token is logged to the dev console on Register (or via Resend Verification below).
Requires an active session cookie (run Login first). 401 unauthenticated if not logged in. Rate limited to 3/hour per user.

This is a browser redirect flow (Google's consent screen), not a JSON API — Postman can't drive it meaningfully. Open this URL in an actual browser to test Google sign-in. Requires GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET to be set in .env.local.
